steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $12x^{3}y+20xy^{2}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $7x^{3}y+9xy^{2}$: Coefficients 7 and 9 cannot come from distributing $4xy$; the outer factor would need to be something other than $4xy$. ✗
  • Choice B — $12x^{2}y+5y$: $4xy \cdot 3x^2 = 12x^3y$, not $12x^2y$ (one power of $x$ is lost); $4xy \cdot 5y = 20xy^2$, not $5y$. ✗
  • Choice C — $12x^{2}y+20xy$: First term has $x^2$ instead of $x^3$; second term $20xy$ is missing one factor of $y$. ✗
  • Choice D — $12x^{3}y+20xy^{2}$: Matches exactly: $12x^3y + 20xy^2$. That is the correct answer. ✓
